text: ‘The Hill of God’ should perhaps be a proper name, ‘Gabaa of God’; it was at Gabaa Saul lived. So in verse 10 below.
This seems to have been a general direction for times of emergency (cf. 13.8), not a command to be observed on the present occasion.

‘Who can tell the parentage of any of them?’; literally, ‘Who is the father of them?’ The phrase is very obscure; the rendering given above assumes that the prophets were notoriously men of low birth, and that the question either expresses surprise (on account of Cis’ wealth, 9.1), or implies that the proceeding is natural (in view of Saul’s obscurity, 9.21).
